analysis.note: Declinable adjective: “born/produced where.” The masculine nominative singular does not straightforwardly agree with neuter tat; without commentary the construction remains syntactically compressed.

verification.detail: compound stem yatraja as Pum/Prathama/Eka: pass; relation tatpurusa: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: Instrumental plural: “with those made of that / identical with that.” Formally a tad + -maya derivative rather than a regular samāsa.
Participial noun/adjective “arhat.” The form can also be read as pañcamī singular of an -at stem, but the negative existential construction favors a genitive possessor/sphere: “of/in an arhat.”
Neuter nominative singular pronoun, taken as the subject of an elliptical negative existential: “that is not.”

verification.detail: compound stem nirodhasamāpatti as Stri/Saptami/Eka: pass; relation tatpurusa: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: Locative singular governed by the repeated negation: “on/in the path.”
Agrees with mārge: “on the supramundane path.”

verification.detail: compound stem lokottara as Pum/Saptami/Eka: pass; relation tatpurusa: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: Repeated negator, here coordinated with ca: “nor.”

justifications.decision: The sequence yatrajas tanmayair is best explained as yatrajaḥ tanmayaiḥ with visarga becoming -s before t. Lexically, yatra is a relative adverb and -ja means “born/produced” as a final compound member, including after adverbial words. Splitting it as yatra jaḥ would leave jaḥ without a suitable meaning in context.
options_considered: emending to a neuter yatrajam to agree with tat
justifications.chosen: yatrajaḥ as a single declinable compound, “where-born / born where.”

evidence.translation: Grammatical/lexical reasoning from the requested reading and dictionary entries for yatra and -ja.

justifications.decision: The form is unambiguously instrumental plural. Since the verse has no finite verb and the surrounding syntax is elliptical, the instrumental is taken as associative rather than as a concrete instrument of action.
options_considered: associative instrumental, “with those made of that”
instrumental of means, “by means of those made of that”
predicate adjective independent of any implied antecedent
justifications.chosen: tanmayaiḥ as an instrumental plural of accompaniment: “with those made of that / identical with that.”

evidence.translation: Dictionary sense of tanmaya: “made up of that, absorbed in or identical with that”; instrumental plural ending -aiḥ.

justifications.decision: The form arhataḥ could formally be genitive or ablative singular for an -at stem, but na tat is most naturally an elliptical negative existential, “that is not,” with arhataḥ marking the possessor or sphere: “that is not in the case of an arhat / in an arhat.”
justifications.chosen: arhataḥ as ṣaṣṭhī singular: “of/in an arhat.”

evidence.translation: Morphology of arhat as an -at participial noun/adjective; syntax of genitive with elliptical existence/possession.

justifications.decision: Both nirodhasamāpattau and mārge lokottare are locative singular forms. The repeated na extends the negation of tat to these loci: “not in cessation-attainment; nor on the supramundane path.”
options_considered: locatives of sphere/location under the negation
mārge lokottare as a separate positive phrase
justifications.chosen: nirodhasamāpattau and mārge lokottare as locative loci of absence.

evidence.translation: Locative singular endings: -au for feminine i-stem samāpatti in compound; -e for masculine a-stems mārga and lokottara.

justifications.decision: The three negators are read cumulatively. The final na ca coordinates the last locative phrase with the preceding exclusions rather than introducing a new positive assertion.
options_considered: only the first na negates tat
final na ca as an unrelated clause
justifications.chosen: na … na … na ca as coordinated negation: “not … not … nor.”

evidence.translation: Lexical force of na as negator and ca as enclitic coordinator.

translation:
Where-born (yatrajaḥ), with those made of that (tanmaya),
that is not in an arhat (arhat);
not in the cessation-attainment (nirodhasamāpatti),
nor on the supramundane path (lokottara mārga).
one_shot_delta: A commentary-blind translation is likely to over-assimilate the bhāṣya reading to the vulgate and insert “other factors such as contact” even though these words are absent from the requested text.

Without commentary, the antecedents of tat and tan- in tanmayaiḥ remain underdetermined; a translation may wrongly make them refer to the immediately visible words rather than to the technical item carried over from the preceding verse.
The masculine yatrajaḥ is syntactically awkward beside neuter tat; without explanatory commentary one may either mistranslate it as agreeing directly with tat or emend it silently.
analyzer_disagreements: The analyzer included the vulgate words anyaiḥ sparśa-ādyaiḥ ca. The requested bhāṣya reading omits these, so they are not analyzed here.

The analyzer split yatra_jaḥ. I treat yatrajaḥ as one compound, since ja in the sense “born/produced” is used as an ifc. element after adverbs; as a separate word jaḥ would most naturally mean the letter ja and would not fit the syntax.
The analyzer lemmatized arhataḥ under the verbal root arh as a present participial form. I record the declined nominal/participial stem arhat, Pum Ṣaṣṭhī Eka, since it is not a finite verb.
The analyzer displayed nirodha as a compound first member and samāpattau as the inflected word. I record the whole inflected compound stem nirodhasamāpatti.
open_questions: The precise antecedent of tat is not recoverable from this isolated line without the preceding verse/commentary.
The precise referent of tan- in tanmayaiḥ is unresolved on grammar and dictionary alone.
The masculine nominative yatrajaḥ does not transparently agree with neuter tat; its exact syntactic role remains uncertain without commentary.
Whether arhataḥ should be taken strictly as genitive or could preserve an ablative nuance cannot be finally settled from the isolated grammar alone.
